# Ogr!

> Ogr! ("Ogre!") is a free, bilingual (Polish/English) retro pixel-art gamebook — interactive fiction that runs entirely in the browser. The player takes on the role of a hero, makes choices, and explores a branching, non-linear story with multiple endings. Progress is saved locally, and the game offers light/dark themes, background music, and a typewriter text effect.

## Play

- [Play Ogr! online](https://ogr-two.vercel.app): Launch the game in any modern browser; choose "New Game" to start or "Continue" to resume saved progress.

## Project

- [Source code on GitHub](https://github.com/Matysiak-Mateusz/ogr): Public repository containing the React + Vite single-page application.
- [Sitemap](https://ogr-two.vercel.app/sitemap.xml): XML sitemap listing indexable URLs.
- [robots.txt](https://ogr-two.vercel.app/robots.txt): Crawler access directives for the site.

## How it works

- [Bilingual content](https://ogr-two.vercel.app): The entire story and interface are available in Polish and English; the language can be switched in-app at any time.
- [Local-first saves](https://ogr-two.vercel.app): Progress is stored in the browser's local storage, so no account or installation is required.
- [Retro presentation](https://ogr-two.vercel.app): Pixel-art visuals, monospace typography, and a typewriter reveal for the narrative text.

## Notes

- Genre: gamebook / interactive fiction.
- Tech stack: React and Vite (single-page application) deployed on Vercel.
- Privacy: the game runs client-side only; player data never leaves the browser.
